safety_signals.meaning: Recovery opportunity; mitigation of damage taken

safety_signals.meaning: Increased resilience; reduced damage sustained from threats

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous enemy spawning; hostile environment; boss threats at stage boundaries

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must progress through nine stages defeating each god manifestation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shop presence; currency system (Zenny); purchasable upgrades

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player flying through stage with satellite support; enemy waves
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Constant shooting; rotatable aim system; projectile avoidance mechanics

environmental_storytelling.example: 29th century Earth transformed into 'Dust World' by Bios communicates catastrophic world-state without exposition
Nine stages each containing one of eight evil gods plus final Bios encounter establishes hierarchical threat structure
Currency system (Zenny) and purchasable items suggest functioning human infrastructure amid apocalypse
Barren wasteland visuals reinforce 'Dust World' destruction narrative throughout progression
Player 1 long-range vs Player 2 short-range weapons establish distinct tactical roles in resistance effort

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Omnidirectional movement with independent rotatable aim; can move in one direction while shooting another
Rapid roll switch presses trigger 'megacrush' attack destroying all on-screen enemies at vitality cost
Provides backup firepower synchronized with player shots; rotatable positioning around character
Spawn throughout stages; fire projectiles requiring evasion; concentrate at stage boundaries as bosses
Stage-concluding threats with presumably unique attack patterns; defeat required for progression

notes: Forgotten Worlds establishes world-state through post-apocalyptic setting (Dust World) rather than explicit faction visuals. Danger signals manifest through enemy projectile patterns and boss encounters. Safety is communicated through shop placement and resource economy. The game's visual communication prioritizes threat/mitigation mechanics over faction color-coding or symbolic markers typical of later shmups. The 29th-century apocalypse setting and continuous enemy presence create implicit danger atmosphere. Limited source documentation on specific visual design choices; analysis inferred from gameplay mechanics and narrative framing.
